title: Dortmanna pusilla
intro: Dortmanna pusilla, also known as Low Sweet Gale, is a small shrub native to northern Europe and Asia, belonging to the family Myricaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing pain and swelling in various conditions. Bioactive constituents of Dortmanna pusilla include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
